title: What is 'Dice'? A Fast-Paced Game of Probability & Payouts
excerpt: Dice is a straightforward casino game where players predict if a generated number falls above or below a chosen target. It's high-speed, transparent, and perfect for testing betting systems.
author: Parker, Game Analyst
metaTitle: How to Play Dice | Probability & Betting Strategy Simulation | Calcasi
metaDescription: Learn the rules of the popular 'Dice' online casino game. Understand win probabilities, adjustable payouts, and how to use our on-site simulator to practice your strategies for free.
content:
Overview: 'Dice' is a modern, ultra-simple gambling game where you wager on the outcome of a random number generator between 1 and 100. It is the gold standard for crypto-native casinos like Stake or Duelbits due to its speed and 'Provably Fair' mechanics.

How to Play: You choose a target number (e.g., 50) and bet on whether the result will be 'Above' or 'Below'. The closer your target is to the extreme ends (1 or 99), the higher the payout but the lower the win probability. You have total control over your risk-to-reward ratio.

Pros:
• Extremely easy to learn: you'll master it in seconds.
• High customizability: set your own odds and house edge.
• Transparency: Most modern Dice games show the cryptographic hash for every roll, ensuring it isn't rigged.

Cons:
• High-speed gameplay can lead to rapid bankroll depletion if you aren't disciplined.
• The simplicity may lack the visual depth and 'story' of complex video slots.

Best For:
• Casual players looking for a quick thrill.
• Beginners who want to understand betting math.
• Strategic players using systems like Martingale or D'Alembert.

Strategy Tip: Many successful players use a 'Low Risk' approach by setting a win probability of 80% or higher. This results in small, frequent wins. Alternatively, you can go for 'Moonshots' with a 1-2% win chance for a 50x-99x payout. Proper bankroll management via Interac or Crypto is recommended to handle the variance.

Summary: Dice offers a pure gambling experience. It's about probability and choice. Start with small units to find your rhythm and discover your favorite risk setting.
